items.title: Elbow plot for customer segments
items.scenario: A retailer fits k-means on scaled RFM features (recency, frequency, monetary) for k = 2..10. Inertia drops sharply from k=2 to k=4, then flattens. The team picks k=4 for marketing tiers even though k=6 has slightly lower inertia.
items.explanation: The elbow bend signals diminishing tightness gains; inertia alone would keep pushing k upward without business justification.

items.title: Silhouette exposes a weak cluster
items.scenario: k-means with k=5 on sensor readings yields overall silhouette 0.42, but cluster 3 averages −0.05. Inspecting cluster 3 reveals it straddles two physical regimes the algorithm merged.
items.explanation: Per-cluster silhouette surfaces local failures that a single global score can hide.

items.title: ARI benchmarks clustering against histology labels
items.scenario: Researchers cluster gene-expression patients with k=4 and compare assignments to pathologist subtypes. ARI = 0.71 vs random baseline near 0. They report NMI and homogeneity alongside for a fuller picture.
items.explanation: External metrics quantify alignment with known biology when labels exist for evaluation—not for tuning on the same holdout.

items.title: Stability across random seeds
items.scenario: A fintech team runs k-means (k=3) with 20 random seeds on the same scaled features. Adjusted Rand Index between pairs of runs ranges 0.55–0.98. Low-stability seeds coincide with borderline silhouette; they standardize on k-means++ and discard outlier seeds.
items.explanation: Agreement across refits is an informal stability check complementing inertia and silhouette.
